evidence: מַתְנִיתִין מַנִּי – רַבָּנַן הִיא
gloss: Whose view is represented by the Mishnah?
notes: Opens an attribution problem by asking which tannaitic position the mishnah follows.

evidence: אֶלָּא אִי אָמְרַתְּ בְּעָשִׁיר וְעָנִי מַחְלוֹקֶת
notes: Introduces a hypothetical reading whose consequences are tested immediately.

evidence: הָא מַנִּי? לָא רַבָּנַן וְלָא רַבִּי אֱלִיעֶזֶר
notes: A pointed version of attribution-questioning, often used as a difficulty against an interpretation.

evidence: אֲמַר לֵיהּ, מַתְנִיתִין דְּאָמַר תְּחִילָּה
אֲמַר לֵיהּ: שָׁאנֵי פּוֹעֵל
אֲמַר לֵיהּ: כֹּל כַּמָּה דְּלָא הָדַר בֵּיהּ
notes: Dialogue marker; in these occurrences it introduces the response to a challenge.

evidence: הָכִי נָמֵי מִסְתַּבְּרָא, דְּקָתָנֵי סֵיפָא
gloss: So too, it is reasonable / this also stands to reason.
notes: Flags support for a proposed answer from the wording of the text.

evidence: דְּקָתָנֵי סֵיפָא: אִם מִשֶּׁנְּתָנָהּ לוֹ
notes: Introduces proof from the wording of the end of a mishnah/baraita.

evidence: תְּחִילָּה בְּסֵיפָא לְמָה לִי?
notes: Asks why a word or clause is necessary; here it probes surplus wording.

evidence: פְּשִׁיטָא, אַף עַל גַּב דְּלָא אָמַר תְּחִילָּה
notes: States an expected default, making the extra wording demand explanation.

evidence: אֶלָּא לָאו הָא קָא מַשְׁמַע לַן
gloss: Rather, is it not teaching us...?
notes: Draws an inference from apparently superfluous wording.

evidence: וְאִידָּךְ תָּנָא סֵיפָא לְגַלּוֹיֵי רֵישָׁא
notes: Elliptical prompt asking how the opposing view handles the evidence.

evidence: מַאי טַעְמָא? הָוֵי תּוֹפֵס לְבַעַל חוֹב
[מַאי טַעְמָא] תַּקִּינוּ רַבָּנַן
notes: A recurring reason-seeking formula; one instance asks for the basis of a legal rule, another for the rationale of four-cubit acquisition.

evidence: אֵיתִיבֵיהּ רָבָא לְרַב נַחְמָן: מְצִיאַת פּוֹעֵל לְעַצְמוֹ
gloss: He raised an objection to him.
notes: Introduces a source as a challenge; tagged as proof because the function is evidentiary.

evidence: בַּמֶּה דְּבָרִים אֲמוּרִים – בִּזְמַן שֶׁאָמַר לוֹ
gloss: In what case were these words said?
notes: A limiting-case formula embedded in the cited baraita.

evidence: שָׁאנֵי פּוֹעֵל, דְּיָדוֹ כְּיַד בַּעַל הַבַּיִת
notes: Distinguishes the cited case from the challenged rule.

evidence: וְהָאָמַר רַב: פּוֹעֵל יָכוֹל לַחֲזוֹר בּוֹ
notes: Challenges an answer with an apparently contradictory amoraic statement.

evidence: וְאִם תֹּאמַר מִשְׁנָתֵינוּ
gloss: And if you would say / object...
notes: Anticipates an unstated difficulty from the Mishnah.

evidence: וְאִי אָמְרַתְּ אַרְבַּע אַמּוֹת שֶׁל אָדָם קוֹנוֹת לוֹ
notes: Sets up a consequence of the prior legal statement, usually to produce a challenge.

evidence: הָכָא בְּמַאי עָסְקִינַן – דְּלָא אֲמַר ״אֶקְנֵי״
gloss: Here, what case are we dealing with?
notes: Resolves a contradiction by narrowing the case under discussion.

evidence: וְאִי תַּקּוּן רַבָּנַן, כִּי לָא אֲמַר מַאי הָוֵי
gloss: What of it? / What difference would it make?
notes: Questions whether the proposed distinction actually matters.

evidence: כֵּיוָן דְּנָפַל עָלֶיהָ גַּלִּי דַּעְתֵּיהּ
notes: Explains why the default acquisition mechanism does not apply in the special case.

evidence: וְהָא בְּכׇל מָקוֹם קָאָמַר
notes: Challenges a limitation by appeal to the wording of a prior statement.

evidence: כׇּל מָקוֹם – לְאֵתוֹיֵי צִידֵּי רְשׁוּת הָרַבִּים
notes: Interprets an expansive term as adding a specific case rather than being fully literal.

evidence: בְּמַאי קָמִיפַּלְגִי? מַר סָבַר
notes: Frames the conceptual basis of an amoraic dispute.

evidence: מַר סָבַר חָצֵר מִשּׁוּם יָדָהּ אִיתְרַבַּאי ... וּמַר סָבַר חָצֵר מִשּׁוּם שְׁלִיחוּת אִיתְרַבַּאי
gloss: One master holds... and one master holds...
notes: Provides the opposed rationales behind a dispute.

evidence: מִי אִיכָּא מַאן דְּאָמַר חָצֵר מִשּׁוּם שְׁלִיחוּת אִיתְרַבַּאי
notes: Expresses incredulity before citing a contradiction.

evidence: וְהָתַנְיָא: ׳בְּיָדוֹ׳ אֵין לִי אֶלָּא יָדוֹ
gloss: But isn’t it taught in a baraita...?
notes: Introduces tannaitic evidence as a challenge.

evidence: אֵין לִי אֶלָּא יָדוֹ, גַּגּוֹ חֲצֵירוֹ וְקַרְפִּיפוֹ מִנַּיִן
gloss: I have only...; from where [do I know]...?
notes: Midrashic expansion formula asking for the source of additional cases.

evidence: תַּלְמוּד לוֹמַר ׳הִמָּצֵא תִמָּצֵא׳ מִכׇּל מָקוֹם
notes: Cites a scriptural phrase as the proof/source.

evidence: וְאִי סָלְקָא דַעְתָּךְ חָצֵר מִשּׁוּם שְׁלִיחוּת אִיתְרַבַּאי
gloss: And if it enters your mind that...
notes: Introduces an assumption whose unacceptable consequence is then shown.

evidence: אִם כֵּן מָצִינוּ שָׁלִיחַ לִדְבַר עֲבֵירָה
notes: Marks the consequence drawn from the stated presumption.

evidence: וְקַיְימָא לַן אֵין שָׁלִיחַ לִדְבַר עֲבֵירָה
gloss: We maintain / it is established for us.
notes: Invokes an accepted rule as a premise for the difficulty.

evidence: הֵיכָא אָמְרִינַן דְּאֵין שָׁלִיחַ לִדְבַר עֲבֵירָה
notes: Introduces a scope-limitation of a known rule; question form leads into an answer.

evidence: אֶלָּא מֵעַתָּה, הָאוֹמֵר לְאִשָּׁה וְעֶבֶד
gloss: If so, from now [one should say]...
notes: Rhetorical challenge by extending an answer to an implausible parallel case.

evidence: הָכִי נָמֵי דְּמִיחַיַּיב שׁוֹלְחָן
gloss: So too? / Would you also say so?
notes: Here used rhetorically within an objection, not as an affirmative answer.

evidence: דִּתְנַן נִתְגָּרְשָׁה הָאִשָּׁה נִשְׁתַּחְרֵר הָעֶבֶד חַיָּיבִין לְשַׁלֵּם
gloss: As we learned in a Mishnah.
notes: Introduces mishnaic evidence supporting an answer.

evidence: מַאי בֵּינַיְיהוּ? אִיכָּא בֵּינַיְיהוּ
gloss: What is the practical difference between them?
notes: Asks for the nafka minah between two explanations.

evidence: אִיכָּא בֵּינַיְיהוּ כֹּהֵן דְּאָמַר לֵיהּ לְיִשְׂרָאֵל
notes: Supplies the practical case distinguishing two views.

evidence: אִי נָמֵי אִישׁ דְּאָמַר לָהּ לְאִשָּׁה
notes: Adds a second example of the same distinction.

evidence: לְעִנְיַן גֵּט כּוּלֵּי עָלְמָא לָא פְּלִיגִי
notes: Defines common ground before isolating the actual disagreement.

evidence: כִּי פְּלִיגִי לְעִנְיַן מְצִיאָה
gloss: When they disagree / their dispute is only...
notes: Narrows the dispute after stating the shared premise.
